reviews.review_description: I think this game would be perfect for multiplayer I haven’t really played I much but I can tell that this game would be perfect for it idk if my freind has the game but me and him are wanting to play mincrfat on vr but we can’t and this game is the closest thing to it that’s easy to get it’s just like the game but with a texture pack also can you make the tools smaller casue it’s kind of annoying to mine in tight spaces also can you make it so we can get rid of items out of are inventory casue idk how to remove it

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you so much for your feedback!

We’re currently working on multiplayer and doing our best to bring it to the game as soon as possible. It’s a complex feature, so it will take some time, but you’ll definitely be able to play together with your friend in the future 🙂

For mining in tight spaces, you can adjust the tool grip position. To do this, hold the grip button on your secondary hand controller and move your main hand controller vertically.

There are also two ways to get rid of unwanted items:

1. Move the item to your hotbar, point at it with the cursor, and hold the trigger. The slot will begin filling with color and the item will be dropped.
2. In the inventory, select the item you want to remove and tap anywhere on the blue background inside the inventory window.

Feel free to join our Discord community as well. There you can follow development progress, chat about the game and other topics, and help shape the future of the game through feedback. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_description: This game is amazing I don't know what all the hate is for also Mojang made a public statement years ago saying that they don't care about people making copies of the Minecraft and would never sue. This game is a one-to-one of Minecraft other than some of the newer things but if you join the discord you will see that all of those things including multiplayer is being implemented soon.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for your review and support! We truly appreciate it 🙂

We’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game. We’ll keep working hard on new updates, bringing more features, improvements, and fresh content in the future.

reviews.review_title: Ok, way more than I was expecting.
reviews.review_description: When I saw videos or ads, they never played the sounds or showed the physical touch (as in interacting with blocks instead of pressing a trigger) so I booted up my headset and hoped for the worse, but it was WAY better than I thought, seriously, it’s really good, this game has potential, it has no end, but it has he he nether! I am also looking forward to multiplayer and more, if the developers respond, just know you are doing great, even though it’s a copy of Minecraft, it’s nice to play Minecraft on vr without going through a 50 step tutorial, I hope you guys don’t get pressured or forced to make more progress on development, you can take your time or do what you want, don’t stress yourself out. I also like the death animations, it’s a touch Minecraft never added for the 16 years of being out just keep trying, also please add iron golems, endermen, blazes, wardens and wither skeletons it would be cool

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your feedback! We’re really glad you had a positive experience 😊 And thanks for your support, we really appreciate it!

We’re constantly working on improving the game and regularly adding new content. So in future updates you can definitely expect the mobs you mentioned, the End, multiplayer, and much more.

If you haven’t joined yet, we’d love to have you in our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu.

Update: Hi! We’ve added iron golems and enchanting in the latest update, along with many other new features and content. Give it a try 🙂

reviews.review_description:
If you don't want to go through the hassle of setting up several different mods to make your PC edition of Minecraft into a proper VR game, then just play Realmcraft. It's pretty much Minecraft re-created for Quest VR.

All the one star reviews complaining that it's a copy of Minecraft must be really bored in their day-to-day lives in order to shill on behalf of a multi-billion dollar corporation like Microsoft.

It's a lot of fun, and worth playing if you enjoy open-world games.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for your review! It’s great to hear that you had such a positive experience with the game and found it fun to play 😊

To stay up to date with development and upcoming features, feel free to join our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. We’d be happy to see you there!

reviews.review_title: This is just breaking copyright laws
reviews.review_description: This game in the pictures looked like actual minecraft, but first, there Is no sprint, second, the noises and sfx are copyright protected, meaning this game can't charge money, and lastly, it just sucks. Dont buy, it's quite literally an illegal game

reviews.review_title: LOVE IT!!!! ❤️ But just one thing
reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing! 🤩 I have been playing Minecraft since I was 7 and I have always been looking for that “playing Minecraft for the first time” feeling and this gave it to me, i thank you so much. 😊 😊😊
You have a lot going on here and I am loving it ❤️, there is just one thing, it would be really cool if you added the create mod into the base game, I have always wanted to do create in vr and it just isn’t easy, so it would be cool if you either added create as a base game feature, or if you added a modding system in game, also servers/multiplayer, other than that you are doing an amazing job keep doing what you’re doing 😘🙏😁

developer_response.body:
Hi!
Thank you so much for your review and support! We really appreciate it! We’re very happy to hear that the game gave you that kind of experience 🙂

Since the game is still in early access, we’re currently focused on adding core features and content. We’re also actively working on multiplayer right now. Once multiplayer and the core functionality are in place, we’re planning to add modding as well 🙂

To stay up to date with development news and the project in general, feel free to join our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_description:
Review:
Loving the game on Quest 3! To make it perfect, please consider adding: 

Multiplayer & Cross-Play to play with friends.

Mini-Games for more fun challenges.

More Texture Packs & Skins for customization.

Coordinates (X, Y, Z) to save locations easily.

The End Portal for endgame progression.

Higher Render Distance (Chunks) to fix the foggy/blurry view.

Thank you developers for your hard work! Keep it up.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your review and suggestions 🙂

We're constantly working on improving the game and adding new content. In fact, many of the features you mentioned are already planned or actively being worked on, such as multiplayer. We'll also make sure to discuss the rest of your suggestions with the team.

By the way, coordinates are already available in the game. You can enable them using the corresponding toggle in the settings menu.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. There you can follow development news, chat with other players, share feedback, and stay up to date with upcoming features. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_description:
Pretty good, I love just add multiplayer, some more original content (New original stuff) and change of structures and maybe a little more original content. (Reworked stuff) such as original crafting recipes, music, and the such. And then you got the THE Minecraft VR alternative.

I think I'll stick around the adventure. ^_~

Btw I did make a review on your I think latest video on YouTube already. I just wanted to give an actual helpful review on the common site.

Feel free to join our Discord community as well, it’s the best place to share feedback and follow the project’s development. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

Once it gets multiplayer now I feel like this is gonna really kick off good

reviews.review_title: Makes a promising impression for the fut
reviews.review_description:
I've only played the original Minecraft very little, but I can still say that Realmcraft feels just like its inspiration.

I've uninstalled it for now and am looking forward to further updates. Especially more craftable items like windows, tables, chairs, etc.

Also, improvements: for example, that sometimes hits on trees with an axe aren't registered.

Addendum: 
I really appreciate the progress the game has made so far. One thing I think could be improved: whenever I die, fall from too great a height, or make other mistakes, a nasty—and above all, loud—"GRKKK" sound plays that is painful to my ears. Is this a bug or intentional?

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for your review and for taking the time to share your feedback 🙂

And thanks for reporting the issue with axe hits. We'll pass this information along to our QA team for investigation.

As for the sound issue, we noticed that you already mentioned it on Discord. Could you please record a short video and post it in the bugs channel or send it directly to the admin? That would help us identify the cause much faster and work on a fix.

Thanks again for helping us improve the game!

reviews.review_title: Instant death turning on. Lost items
reviews.review_description:
Very very mad about how I turned the game on last time to play. All I saw was a stone granite block in front of me and then died instantly. I had diamonds, all my armor, iron tools and weapons. Lost them all and have no idea where I died in the game to go retrieve them.

It was several weeks ago so I don't remember what the cause of death stated, everything stated in my review is all the information I have. 

But now I have another issue with the game. I was mining for a long time and had a lot of coal, iron, copper and gold. I took all of that and went to my chest. Stored it all in the chest. Closed the game out and shut the Oculus down. Now I just went back into the game to play again and everything I stored in the chest is gone. All the coal, iron, copper and gold is no longer in my inventory.

05/06 revision: Something happened with the last update that's not in the notes. Previously when I broke a glass block I placed down I would receive it back in my inventory. But now when I break a glass block it doesn't go in my inventory. Terrible change and big miss on the announcements with the last revision. Plus there isn't any method to directly contact the admin of the game to bring these issues to.

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear you've run into this issue.

As mentioned in the Update 1.0.1 notes, we recently added enchanting to the game. Glass blocks now require a tool with the Silk Touch enchantment in order to be collected. Without Silk Touch, the glass will simply break and won't be returned to your inventory.

And yes, you can contact us directly through our Discord community. It's the best place to report bugs, share feedback, and communicate with the team. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu.

reviews.review_description: First off, this is an amazing game, but it can get boring after a while. To be fair this is a semi new game, it doesn’t even have every mob in the game yet, one of the main reasons Minecraft and all other sandbox/building games won’t get boring fast is because of mods, car mods, Star Wars mods, even gun mods! I think a lot of people will buy the game if you can download mods, and if you do please make some be able to be downloaded some on the VR not everyone has a PC. Again these are just recommendations for a great VR game. Edit: another thing, I love how this games developers actually respond back and listen to the community.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you for your review!

Many players have been asking for mod support, and it’s definitely something we plan to add in the future. Right now, our top priority is multiplayer. As soon as multiplayer is released, we plan to start working on mod support.

We agree that mods can greatly expand the experience and add a lot of new possibilities for players, so it’s an important part of our long term roadmap.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. There we share development progress, post news about upcoming features, and discuss the future of the project with players. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there 🙂

reviews.review_description:
It's a great Minecraft VR experience, and it skips all the bs steps quest craft makes you do, but please make it have original like unique mobs or items. im worried you might get sued over copyright infringement, but here are some ideas and things i think would make it more enjoyable

1. Bigger and deeper caves
2. Mobs Minecraft won't add like sharks or other things Minecraft is to scared to add
3. Unique and original items 
4. A ending (im not sure if the game has one im not that far into it)

Im not someone who cares that much about multiplayer, but it would be cool overall this game is very enjoyable especially for the price it is the best Minecraft clone on quest by a landslide and i'm glad this game is getting constant updates.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you so much for your feedback and suggestions!

Since the game is still in early access, we’re currently focused on adding core features. However, once the foundation is fully in place, we do plan to expand the game with more original content, so it’s very possible that unique mobs, items, and other features will appear in future updates.

We’re also planning to add a final dimension and a final boss in future updates, improve caves, and continue expanding the overall experience.

Feel free to join our Discord community as well. There you can follow development progress and stay up to date with project news. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_title: You can't even do simple redstone.
reviews.review_description: Pistons don't work, target blocks don't work and you can't even turn off redstone torches. Redstone aside it's a pretty good mc copy. The mobs look a bit baby-ish though. Oh yeah, ADD MULTIPLAYER!!!!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback.

The game is still in early access, so not all blocks and mechanics are available yet. At the moment, only part of the redstone functionality has been implemented, but we’re continuously working on improving the game and adding new content with future updates.

As for the mobs, we’re planning to add modding support in the future. This will allow players to use various mods, including texture packs and custom mob models.

Multiplayer is also currently in development. We’re doing our best to bring it to the game as soon as possible, though it’s a complex feature and will take some time.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. There you can chat with other players, stay up to date with development news, follow our progress, and more. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_description: As a dedicated Minecraft veteran, I can confidently say that this is an amazing recreation of Minecraft! As soon as I loaded into the game, I knew this would be different than some of those junk Minecraft servers! Based on the gameplay and everything I can confidently say this is a five out of five and the only thing that could make it even better than it is now is if it had a multiplayer option. If you’re looking for something for VR, that is very similar to Minecraft but doesn’t require you to get a bunch of extra stuff you need to do, this is a perfect option for you!

reviews.review_description:
The new updates have been great. Love every single one. I don’t have discord, so I’m gonna drop some stuff here.

There’s a bug with the sword and a couple other tools where it doesn’t allow me to adjust its position in my hand. The sword in particular is hovering above my fist.

Also, it would be cool if I could punch blocks with both hands, not just my dominant one.

Thirdly and most importantly, I think there are some out-of-the-box possibilities here. This is a game with the foundations to be an incredibly detailed block based survival game. I think that there should be a lot of effort put towards making the game super immersive with what you can do. Imagine what you could do In games like Minecraft if you weren’t limited by a controller! Petting animals, waving at villagers, stoking things in a furnace or on a campfire with a stick, is all just the tip of the iceberg. I’ll leave the bigger ideas up to you. Very excited for this games future!!

EDIT: there is no way to get rid of excess items form the inventory. I’ll find myself stuck with a full inventory having to make a chest to put junk in. I want to be able to just throw it outside the inventory menu and have it pop out onto the floor. 


All in all it’s worth a buy!!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed and helpful feedback! We’re really glad to hear that you’re enjoying the game 🙂

To adjust the position of tools, hold the grip button on your secondary hand controller and move your main hand controller vertically. Please note that this applies only to tools, the grip height for the sword itself cannot be adjusted.

Regarding dual hand block breaking, this used to be available, but with the addition of physics-based block breaking using controller swings, it was removed due to certain limitations. However, we may revisit this in the future.

We’re also planning to further improve immersion and continue adding features that enhance VR interaction. Since the game is still in early access, we’re currently focused on core mechanics, blocks, and overall stability, but immersive features remain an important direction for us moving forward.

There are two ways to get rid of unwanted items:

1. If the item is in your hotbar, hover over it and hold the trigger. The icon will fill with green and the item will be dropped.
2. If the item is in your inventory, select it and then tap anywhere on the blue background of the inventory window to drop it.

reviews.review_description: When there's multiplayer I'll rate it a 5 because I wanna play it with my best friend but other then that it feels so nice and immersive, but now let's move onto the things that I dont like, so I cant swim immersively I don't know why they haven't added that yet and I cant crouch anymore when I press the button to crouch it just puts me in third person and I want a option to change the button layout for people who wanna play it but don't like the current button layout

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback!

We’re currently working on multiplayer, and it will definitely be added in future updates.

We will also take your suggestion regarding immersive swimming into consideration for future updates.

To crouch, you need to press the joystick on your main hand controller. What you described (being switched to third person) happens when pressing the joystick on the secondary hand controller, which activates photo mode.

Regarding button layout customization, we’ve discussed this with the team and this option is likely to be added in the future as well.

If you’d like to stay up to date with development and share your suggestions, feel free to join our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. We’d be happy to see you there!

Hi! Thank you for your feedback.

We completely understand why multiplayer is important to you. In fact, we’re already working on it and doing our best to bring it to the game as soon as possible. It’s a complex feature and will take some time, but it is definitely planned.

You’ll be able to play together with your kids in the future, and we’re looking forward to making that possible 🙂

To avoid missing multiplayer news and development updates, feel free to join our Discord community. We’ll be sharing progress updates and details about the feature there. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_description: I got a VR mainly because of Blade and Sorcery and Minecraft. However it was a hassle to connect my Headset to ViveCraft, so when I don't want to deal with it, but still want Minecraft in VR, this is an amazing alternative. I hardly care it's in early access, it's got the same stuff I wanted from minecraft in VR, just building a cozy place and living there. It's super fun and while it's definitely easy to tell it's not minecraft, that doesn't bother me. One thing I would like is dual hand breaking, where you can use both hands at the same time to break the block, and to dual wield weapons and tools for this purpose. I'm not a fan of the Bedrock Style Offhand Restrictions, so making it like java where everything can go into the offhand would be greatly appreciated.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you for your review! Great to hear you’re enjoying the game and having that kind of experience 🙂

Right now we’re focused on adding core mechanics and blocks, as well as working on multiplayer. That said, thanks for your suggestion regarding offhand usage, and we’ll definitely take it into consideration for future updates.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. There you can chat with other players and share your ideas. We also share development updates there. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!

reviews.review_description: This game makes me feel like the first time I played Minecraft as a kid when it was first released on pc. I was mind blown by the biomes and animals and being able to create a world all your own. Now you get to step into your world and that somehow just makes up for the lack of imagination I have now as an adult and I get to finally feel like a kid for a while again.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thank you so much for your review. It’s great to hear that the game brought back those feelings and gave you that kind of experience 😊

That kind of feedback really motivates us to keep improving the game🙂

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. There you can chat with other players, suggest ideas, and vote on upcoming features. We also share development updates there. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. See you there!

reviews.review_description:
This is a review of the game in its Early Access state as of April 2026.

Some of the reviews are showing hate on this game due to how close it is to Minecraft. Well, go play the official Minecraft release on the Quest platform. Oh wait, there isn't one.

With that said,  think it's easy to assume it's just some ripoff cash grab. But after you start to play the game, you'll immediately realize that it's not just some copy/paste job. It's been built from the ground up, and it's built with the goal of giving an authentic Minecraft experience natively on the Quest platform.

The game runs silky smooth, has plenty of customization options, and has its own identity and gives its own vibe. You've also got Architect mode for building worlds you can share, and a Worlds option to download worlds other people have creatd.

It's extremely solid and smooth, in fact I don't really think I've encountered any real bugs. The only slightly finicky thing that comes to mind is swinging your tools in a 1-block space is difficult to break blocks. But you always have the option to use the point and click option to remove blocks at any time.

Arachnophobia mode is a great option there for people who hate spiders.  On-the-fly difficulty adjustment is awesome too, there may be cases where you only want to enable monsters in order to kill them for certain items and make they go away again.

There are still some tables that aren't functional yet, surely they'll be added in future versions. And it would be nice to see some more types of mounts for land or even air travel (getting ahead of myself,  I know).

Bottom line, it's fun, solid, runs super smooth, customizable, and is clearly being made by FANS of this Minecraft style of gameplay. As a result, you're getting a really great game.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you for taking the time to leave such a detailed review. We really appreciate it and are happy to hear that you’re enjoying the experience 🙂

In future updates, we will be adding new mounts, functionality for blocks, and many other features, so the game will continue to improve over time.

Regarding swinging in tight spaces: you can adjust the tool grip height. To do this, hold the grip button on your secondary hand controller and move your main hand controller vertically.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. See you there!

reviews.review_description: As a minecraft fanatic, this is my go to game to get away from things. The graphics and creativity is beyond imaginable. We just need one thing MULTIPLAYER!!!! 😩🙌

reviews.review_description:
I was skeptical, yet for the price I figured why not? And duuuuuuude for an early access game, I am very impressed. 
Since the advent of VR, I have always wanted to play Minecraft, without the 50 plus step guide to do it.
Bear in mind it is an early access game, so it's gonna have some bugs.
Definitely worth a go. 

Now there I was happily mining away, I turned round and AHHHHHHHHHHH 😱......A cow was behind me.
Yep that's right, I almost made bone meal in my pants because of a cow 🐮 

Well done developers, well done indeed. 
Keep at it, look forward to future updates 😘

developer_response.body:
Sounds like that cow really caught you off guard 😄

Thank you for your review. Really glad you’re enjoying the game! Hope you’ll enjoy future updates as well.

If you haven’t joined yet, we’d love to have you in our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there 🙂

reviews.review_title: The best VR clone made so far!! 👍
reviews.review_description: Honestly, this game is what i wanted and more. It's not exactly to the T, Minecraft but....it is in fact so close I couldn't tell the difference except for the mobs. But the actual imersive gameplay is sooo good. This will be my new go-to game for 2026. Especially if they put co-op or multiplayer mode in this. The best part is this game is running butter smooth for me on standalone. Good job devs, if multiplayer comes I will definitely recommend and gift this one fast.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for such an awesome review!
Really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game.

Multiplayer is already in development and will definitely be coming in the future. 
We share development updates, chat with the community, and run polls for upcoming features on our Discord, including news and progress on multiplayer as it moves forward.

You can find the invite link on our official website (linked on the game’s store page) or in the bottom-left corner of the game’s main menu. Would be great to have you there!

reviews.review_description:
That's basically Minecraft VR, runs smooth, looks good, what else I can say?


To the developers.
I would appreciate if you consider adding a few things to the game:
- Add a sprint
- Add an option to bind/rebind buttons
- Add an option to craft new things manually. I mean by placing them in the right order on the crafting table like in the original game. Currently it's autocraft only.

Also I know you're working on the multiplayer and it's a time consuming business, but for the future please consider a few more things:
- Adding some kind of inventory tweaks that allows to stack and sort items in the backpack and chests automatically.
- Maybe a map and a clock would be nice? (since people use addons of that kind in the original game anyway)
- I am a big fan of industrial craft and similar mods and would be happy if you somehow add it as a game mode
- Addons feature would be nice too

And the last thing. I guess it's coming from the original Minecraft VR, but the range of melee weapons auto-attack is too far and it really feels like cheating since your sword becomes a long range weapon - you literally destroy everything from the long distance where it can't hurt you. The problem is the close combat range is the same as the building range(which is obviously far). So you should kinda adjust it a bit, I mean the combat range only, make it separate from the building one.

developer_response.body:
Hello! Thank you so much for such detailed feedback. We’re really glad to hear that you’re enjoying the game!

I’ll make sure to pass all your suggestions to the team for discussion. Some of the things you mentioned have already been considered and will be added over time.

By the way, sprinting is already in the game! To use it, push your movement stick forward twice quickly. The speed boost is subtle compared to flying in Creative mode, so it may not feel very noticeable. We added special particles to indicate sprinting, which you can enable in the settings under “Acceleration Particles.”

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to become part of our Discord community. That’s where we share project news, sneak peeks of upcoming updates, and run polls on upcoming features. You can find the invite link on our official website, which can be found on the game’s store page, or directly in the game at the bottom-left corner of the main menu.

We’d love to see you there!

reviews.review_description: I love the graphics and interactivity...the game looks amazing on the Quest 3!!!  I also appreciate all the game mode options. You can play Survival, Architect or Creative and the devs are quick to fix bugs via  their Discord page. It's the perfect "Chill" game!! Keep up the good work Devs!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to leave a review.

We’re sorry to hear about the issues you’ve been experiencing with the minecart –that’s definitely frustrating, especially after spending so much time building your track system.

Could you please let us know what exactly isn’t working with the minecart right now? Any specific details would really help us investigate and understand what might have gone wrong.

We may have missed your emails, and we apologize for that. For faster communication, we highly recommend joining our Discord community, where you can report issues directly and get quicker responses.

You can find the invite link on our official website (linked on the store page) or directly in the game at the bottom-left corner of the main menu. We’d really appreciate you stopping by so we can look into this together.

reviews.review_description:
Enjoying the game so far, but have a couple of requests for control options, both regarding the right joystick...

Rather than the "turn speed" variable for looking left and right, could it just be a proportional control (ie. the further you push the stick the faster you turn)? With the turn speed set high it's very jarring to use, and set low it's just too slow; and neither setting works for every situation! Perhaps the slider in options could be to set maximum turn speed? I'll have another play trying the snap turn option...

Also, I'd love to be able to look up and down using the right stick! I know this sounds daft as you can just look up and down with your head in VR, but I like playing MC sitting down (well, slouching on the sofa...!), and you just can't look down in that position!🥺

With those two control changes in place I would find the game a lot more enjoyable to play; hope this is something that can be addressed in a future update. Oh, and someone mentioned there's no sprint button? That would be a nice addition too!😊

Other than that, it's wonderful seeing the world in VR!!😁

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for such a detailed and useful review. We really appreciate the time you took to share all of this feedback.

We’ll definitely discuss your control suggestions with the team, and some of them might make their way into future updates. Regarding sprinting, all the buttons are currently assigned, so we can’t dedicate a separate one at the moment. We understand that pushing the stick forward twice to sprint isn’t ideal, so we’ll continue looking for a solution that feels comfortable for everyone.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to hop into our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom-left corner of the main menu. We’d love to see you there!

DEFINITELY keep updating this game, very wonderful how accurate this is. Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
I've played it on both Steam and Meta Quest for around 50 hours total, and here's what's currently in the game and what I think of it:

- The game currently has 3 game modes: Survival, Creative, and Architect. And multiplayer is expected to arrive in 3 to 5 months, according to one of the developers.

- It doesn't have things like the end, multiplayer, and enchanting. However, all of them will be included in future updates.

- Some blocks don't work just yet, like the enchanting table, but as I mentioned, they will be functional eventually.

I have been playing the mobile version since late 2016 or early 2017 and started playing the VR version in early May of 2025.

The developers are really nice and post progress on the game's updates on their server.

I highly recommend giving it a try. I tried it and didn't have a single regret!

In addition to that, it's about to exit the "early access" stage with the 1.0.0 update.

(I reviewed this game around May 15th, 2025, but I edited it now.)

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your amazing review 😊
We’re so happy that you’ve been with us for so long and truly appreciate your support. It means a lot to us. We’ll keep working hard to release exciting updates and keep making the game enjoyable for our players.

reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing it’s only missing one thing !
MUTIPLAYER!!!!! Please add or at least tell us you’re working on it! PLEASE PLEASE PLEASE ADD MULTIPLAYER! Thank you so much 😁

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for your feedback.

Yes, we’ve already started working on multiplayer 🙂 It’s a complex feature and will take some time, but it will definitely be added in the future.

To stay up to date with multiplayer news and development progress, we invite you to join our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website (listed on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom-left corner of the main menu. We’d be glad to see you there!

reviews.review_title: The best game I’ve been looking for.
reviews.review_description:
This is the best game I’ve been looking for.  
You won’t regret buying it. It’s still in Early Access so there’s no multiplayer yet, but it seems like it will be added in the future, so that’s not a problem.

Since it’s still in Early Access and the developers seem to want feedback, I’ll share what I noticed while playing.  
First, I’d like a sprint option — sorry if it already exists.  
Also, doors can’t be opened both ways; their opening direction is fixed.  
Additionally, picture frames and paintings can’t be placed (though this will probably be addressed in the full release).

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thank you so much for your review. We’re glad to hear you’re enjoying the game 😊

The game does have a sprint feature. To sprint, push forwards on your movement stick twice. When you start sprinting, particles will appear to show that you are running. You can enable them in the settings under “acceleration effect.”

Doors can open in different directions depending on placement. When placing a door, try clicking on the right or left side of the block you are attaching it to. This will determine which way the door opens.

Some blocks are currently unavailable because the game is still in Early Access. Either they are not placeable yet or are decorative only. They will be fully implemented in future updates.

If you have any more suggestions, or just want to chat with the team and other players, feel free to join our Discord community. You can find the invite link on our official website, which can be found on the game’s store page, or directly in the game at the bottom-left corner of the main menu. We would love to see you there!

reviews.review_description: Underwhelming not worth it and afterwards it would not let me refund I spent $10 on this piece of trash

developer_response.body:
Hi, we’re sorry to hear you had this experience.

We’re not able to process refunds directly, as they are handled by the Meta Store. Please contact Meta Support, and they will be able to assist you with your refund request.

If you’re open to it, we’d still appreciate hearing what exactly didn’t work for you in the game. Your feedback helps us improve the experience for all players.

reviews.review_title: Why you should buy this over Questcraft.
reviews.review_description:
First off, and the biggest difference you'll notice is purely just ease of access. You can buy this, download it, and play it. Questcraft is a headache. If you haven't gone through doing the who sideloading stuff, not only do you need to do that, connect your account, modify settings to even play it, but mainly the load times. Anyone who has played questcraft knows the load times are ridiculous and insane. Call me crazy but being able to take a break and go back to a game without a 15+ minute load screen is almost mandatory for me at this point.

Now for some smaller advantages it has over questcraft is definitely the world sharing, being able to launch the game and load into a massive city world and really see what the game can be, helps sell it easily.

There's definitely still some drawbacks, from not being fully up to date to feeling like a cheap mobile port (the ui and menus are ugly, your character can literally where a copyrighted minecraft shirt, a lot of the bricks are obvious to tell what they are) at the end of the day it feels like an slightly earlier version of minecraft but with a texture pack. I'd even argue the controls are actually better. (Also for anyone wondering the actual render and draw distance seems very similar between both, with both allowing you to expand it at a performance cost)

I'd say the first update i want to see is simply a nicer ui. For larger updates, obviously catching up to standard minecraft, but aside from that id say basic mod support, downloadable texture packs, downloadable skins, and obviously like everyone else already said multi-player.

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for taking the time to write such a detailed and thoughtful review. We really appreciate it 🙂

The game is still in early access, but we're actively working on it and regularly adding new content and features. Many aspects of the game, including the UI, may evolve and improve over time as development continues.

Right now, we're focused on multiplayer development. Once multiplayer is in place, we plan to start working on mod support as well.

If you haven’t joined yet, feel free to join our Discord community. There you can follow development progress, stay up to date with upcoming features, share feedback, and chat with other players. You can find the invite link on our official website (on the game’s store page) or directly in the game at the bottom left corner of the main menu. Hope to see you there!
